concerns.text: T024 implements a 'paired t-test' on squared errors. This contradicts FR-005 and SC-002 in spec.md which explicitly mandate a 'single-sample t-test' comparing CNN error to baseline error. The task silently relaxes the statistical hypothesis test required by the spec.

T030 attempts to satisfy SC-005 by calculating 'Pearson correlation' or IoU. SC-005 strictly requires an 'IoU ≥ 0.4' against manually annotated boundaries OR an 'expert review report'. The plan's suggestion of a correlation proxy is a semantic weakening of the success criterion and does not satisfy the spec's mandatory threshold.

T031 implements sensitivity analysis using the 'median predicted strength' as the binarization threshold. While this matches FR-007, the plan.md (Phase 3 Task 3.5) explicitly instructs to use the 'Ground Truth Median'. The task follows the spec but creates a conflict with the plan's instruction, risking implementation drift if the plan is followed instead of the spec. The task must explicitly resolve this by adhering to the spec's FR-007 definition.

T032 specifies using 'Monte Carlo Dropout (30 samples)' to calculate confidence intervals. FR-008 requires a '95% confidence interval'. Without a task or plan section explicitly validating that 30 samples are sufficient to achieve 95% coverage, this implementation detail risks failing the success criterion. The task assumes a parameter value not verified against the spec's coverage requirement.
